title: Information risk management for financial institutions

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for §5.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that BAIT/KAIT §5 (Identity & access management) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.28.16: Information risk management for financial institutions.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.28.16' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: Windows Security Event Logs, critical infrastructure system logs, BSI IT-Grundschutz compliance scans, SIEM correlation events, vulnerability scan outputs, change management records, incident response logs, security audit evidence
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
spl:
index=risk sourcetype="bait:irm" earliest=-365d entity="*bank*"
| stats latest(inherent_risk) as ir, latest(residual_risk) as rr by process_id
| where rr>ir*0.6
| table process_id, ir, rr
description: Demonstrates alignment with BAIT/KAIT expectations for information risk management for financial institutions using auditable Splunk evidence.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for BAIT/KAIT compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes risk and sourcetypes bait:irm. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Maintain `de_kritis_assets.csv` with sector classification and KRITIS identifiers. (2) Link ITSI services to KRITIS objects for business impact overlays. (3) Export monthly control posture snapshots for BSI engagement. (4) Tune thresholds with enterprise risk management and OT security owners. Indexes required: `index=risk`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=bait:irm`.
visualization: Matrix (host x module), Bar chart (severity), Table (controls). Key fields: `ir`, `process_id`, `rr`.
knownFalsePositives: Grundschutz checks during quarterly reviews, controlled exception cycles, or scheduled refreshes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Windows Security Event Logs, critical infrastructure system logs, BSI IT-Grundschutz compliance scans, SIEM correlation events, vulnerability scan outputs, change management records, incident response logs, security audit evidence.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain `de_kritis_assets.csv` with sector classification and KRITIS identifiers. (2) Link ITSI services to KRITIS objects for business impact overlays. (3) Export monthly control posture snapshots for BSI engagement. (4) Tune thresholds with enterprise risk management and OT security owners.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=risk sourcetype="bait:irm" earliest=-365d entity="*bank*"
| stats latest(inherent_risk) as ir, latest(residual_risk) as rr by process_id
| where rr>ir*0.6
| table process_id, ir, rr
```

Understanding this SPL

**Information risk management for financial institutions (BAIT/KAIT)** — Demonstrates alignment with BAIT/KAIT expectations for information risk management for financial institutions using auditable Splunk evidence.

Documented **Data sources**: Windows Security Event Logs, critical infrastructure system logs, BSI IT-Grundschutz compliance scans, SIEM correlation events, vulnerability scan outputs, change management records, incident response logs, security audit evidence. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: risk; **sourcetype**: bait:irm. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=risk, sourcetype="bait:irm", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **process_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Filters the current rows with `where rr>ir*0.6` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
Output fields: ir, process_id, rr.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the BSI compliance report and the same reporting window the security team used on paper. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Matrix (host x module), Bar chart (severity), Table (controls).
grandmaExplanation: We show alignment with BAIT/KAIT expectations for information risk management for financial institutions using auditable Splunk evidence. That matches the critical-infrastructure and reporting work we are asked to do in the region we operate in.
